name: Labelbox
what_it_does: Enterprise data annotation and labeling platform for building ML training datasets. Labelbox provides labeling tools for images, video, text, audio, and geospatial data with quality control workflows, model-assisted labeling (MAL), and active learning integration. Includes a Python SDK for programmatic project management, annotation export, and workflow automation. Used by ML teams to create high-quality labeled datasets for model training.
use_cases: Automate annotation dataset creation for agent fine-tuning — use Labelbox Python SDK to create projects, upload data, and export annotations programmatically
Implement active learning loops where agents identify uncertain predictions for human review and relabeling via Labelbox API
Use model-assisted labeling (MAL) to have agents pre-label data and human reviewers correct mistakes — accelerating annotation throughput
Quality control agent output annotations by routing them through Labelbox's consensus and review workflows
Export approved annotations in standard formats (COCO, Pascal VOC, JSON) for agent model training pipelines
not_for: Simple text annotation projects — Label Studio open source is sufficient and free for basic NLP annotation needs
Teams wanting fully automated labeling without human review — Labelbox is a human-in-the-loop platform; use model inference directly for auto-labeling
Small annotation projects under budget — Labelbox is enterprise-priced; Scale AI or Toloka are better for one-time annotation projects
best_when: Enterprise ML teams needing a managed platform for ongoing data labeling with quality control, model-assisted labeling, and active learning integration.
avoid_when: One-time annotation projects or small teams without ongoing labeling needs — Labelbox's enterprise pricing and complexity isn't justified for small-scale annotation.

auth.notes: API key for Python SDK and REST/GraphQL API access. Key in Labelbox account settings. Passed as Authorization header. Single key grants full account access — no scope granularity.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free tier: up to 5 users, 10,000 data rows/month, limited automation features
pricing.paid_tiers: Starter from $148/month; Team from $337/month; Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: Free tier is limited for production annotation workflows. Production ML annotation typically requires Starter or higher. Enterprise adds SSO, advanced automation, and SLA.

performance.rate_limits: GraphQL API rate limits per plan; large data uploads require batch operations

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: GraphQL API returns structured errors. Python SDK raises typed exceptions. Webhook payloads for async annotation events. Upload errors include data row ID for tracking which assets failed.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Data row creation can check for existing external IDs — use external_id field for idempotent data uploads. Label creation is not idempotent — duplicate labels can be created. Annotation export is idempotent (read-only).
known_agent_gotchas: Labelbox primary API is GraphQL — agents using REST patterns must adapt to GraphQL query syntax via the Python SDK
Data upload is asynchronous — uploaded data rows may not be immediately available for labeling or export; poll status or use webhooks
Annotation schema (ontology) must be defined in Labelbox UI or API before uploading data — schema-first design required
Model-assisted labeling (MAL) requires specific prediction format that varies by annotation type (bounding box, polygon, classification) — validate format carefully
Label export includes all historical annotations — agents must filter for the latest/approved annotations from the export data
Webhook payload verification uses HMAC signatures — agents receiving webhooks must validate signatures to prevent spoofing

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 Type II, GDPR, HIPAA compliant. HTTPS enforced. SSO for enterprise. API key with no scope granularity is a concern for shared access. EU data residency available. HIPAA BAA available for healthcare annotation.
